cli_check.what_to_scan: Project root and docs/ for AI literacy training documentation
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Run 'ai-comply fix --literacy' to generate AI Literacy Policy template with all required sections.
cli_check.warning_message: No AI Literacy documentation found. Article 4 requires all organizations using AI to ensure staff have sufficient AI literacy. This obligation is already in force since February 2, 2025.
negative_signals: No AI literacy or training documentation found
Document exists but missing required sections (scope, levels, schedule)
No training records or completion tracking mechanism
positive_signals: Document contains 'training level' or 'AI literacy' sections
Training record template or completion tracking

cli_check.what_to_scan: Dependency tree and import statements for prohibited AI practice patterns
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Run 'ai-comply fix --art5' to generate Prohibited Practices Screening template. Review each AI system against 8 prohibited categories.
cli_check.warning_message: No Article 5 Prohibited Practices Screening found. Prohibited practices carry the highest fines (€35M / 7% turnover) and are already in force since February 2, 2025.
negative_signals: No Art. 5 screening documentation
Imports from facial recognition mass-scraping libraries (e.g., face_recognition with scraping patterns)
Social scoring calculation patterns in code
Criminal profiling logic without objective fact inputs
positive_signals: No imports from known prohibited-use libraries
Screening report with sign-off and date

cli_check.what_to_scan: Code patterns indicating subliminal or manipulative AI techniques
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Review flagged code for compliance with Art. 5(1)(a). Add user control mechanisms and transparent disclosure of AI-driven recommendations.
cli_check.warning_message: Potential subliminal/manipulative AI technique pattern detected. Art. 5(1)(a) prohibits AI that deploys techniques beyond a person's consciousness to materially distort behavior.
negative_signals: Import of dark pattern or persuasion profiling libraries
A/B testing on vulnerable user segments without consent safeguards
Algorithmic content recommendations designed to maximize engagement without user control

cli_check.what_to_scan: Code patterns exploiting vulnerable groups (age, disability, economic)
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Remove vulnerability-based targeting. Add safeguards for vulnerable user groups. Implement age verification where applicable.
cli_check.warning_message: Potential exploitation of vulnerable groups detected. Art. 5(1)(b) prohibits AI exploiting vulnerabilities due to age, disability, or social/economic situation.
negative_signals: User segmentation by age/disability/economic status for differential AI behavior
Targeting minors or elderly with AI-driven persuasion
Exploiting economic vulnerability indicators in AI recommendations
positive_signals: No targeting based on disability/economic status

cli_check.what_to_scan: Social scoring patterns aggregating behavior across contexts
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Ensure scoring is context-specific. Do not aggregate behavior across unrelated domains. Do not restrict service access based on behavioral scores.
cli_check.warning_message: Potential social scoring system detected. Art. 5(1)(c) prohibits evaluating persons based on social behavior leading to detrimental treatment in unrelated contexts.
negative_signals: Cross-context behavior aggregation into unified score
User scores affecting unrelated service access
Social behavior data merged across domains
positive_signals: Scoring limited to relevant context only
No access restriction based on behavioral scores

cli_check.what_to_scan: Criminal risk profiling based on demographics or personality
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Remove demographic/personality-based criminal profiling. If risk assessment needed, base on objective verifiable facts only.
cli_check.warning_message: Potential criminal risk profiling detected. Art. 5(1)(d) prohibits assessing criminal risk based solely on profiling or personality traits.
negative_signals: Criminal risk prediction based on demographics
Personality trait-based risk scoring
Profiling without objective fact basis
positive_signals: Risk assessment based on objective verifiable facts only

cli_check.what_to_scan: Untargeted facial image collection or scraping patterns
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Remove facial image scraping. Use only consensual, targeted facial data collection with documented lawful basis.
cli_check.warning_message: Potential untargeted facial scraping detected. Art. 5(1)(e) prohibits creating or expanding facial recognition databases through untargeted scraping.
negative_signals: Web scraping for facial images (BeautifulSoup/Scrapy + face detection)
Bulk image download from social media
CCTV feed processing for facial recognition database building
positive_signals: Facial data processing with explicit consent only

cli_check.what_to_scan: Codebase for emotion recognition in workplace/education contexts
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Remove emotion recognition from all workplace and educational applications immediately. Medical/safety exceptions require documented justification.
cli_check.warning_message: CRITICAL [OBL-002f]: Emotion recognition detected in workplace/education context. This is a PROHIBITED PRACTICE under Article 5(1)(f). Maximum fine: €35M / 7% turnover.
negative_signals: Emotion detection libraries (Affectiva, Azure Face emotion, fer, deepface emotion) imported in HR/LMS/workplace code
Sentiment analysis applied to employee communications (Slack, email)
Emotion scoring in performance review systems
positive_signals: Emotion detection explicitly disabled in HR/LMS modules
Configuration blocking emotion features in workplace context

cli_check.what_to_scan: Biometric categorization by sensitive characteristics
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Remove sensitive characteristic inference from biometric processing. Do not classify individuals by protected characteristics using biometric data.
cli_check.warning_message: Potential biometric categorization by sensitive characteristics. Art. 5(1)(g) prohibits using biometric data to categorize persons by race, political opinions, religion, sex life, or sexual orientation.
negative_signals: Biometric data used to infer race, religion, political opinion
Facial analysis for ethnicity classification
Voice analysis for sensitive characteristic inference
positive_signals: No sensitive attribute inference from biometric data

cli_check.what_to_scan: Project documentation for risk management system artifacts
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Run 'ai-comply fix --risk-mgmt' to generate Risk Management System template with all Art. 9 required sections.
cli_check.warning_message: No Risk Management System documentation found. Article 9 requires providers of high-risk AI to establish and maintain a continuous risk management system.
negative_signals: No risk management documentation
Risk document exists but missing misuse scenarios
No evidence of testing or review dates
Risk management is only in TODO/draft state
positive_signals: Document contains sections: identified risks, misuse scenarios, mitigations, test results
Evidence of periodic risk review (dates, sign-offs)

cli_check.what_to_scan: Risk identification and analysis documentation
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Create risk register documenting: each identified risk, severity, likelihood, affected persons, mitigation measures.
cli_check.warning_message: No risk identification/analysis documentation found. Art. 9(2)(a) requires identifying and analyzing known and reasonably foreseeable risks.
negative_signals: No risk register or risk analysis documentation
Risks identified without severity ratings
No mitigation measures for identified risks
positive_signals: Severity and likelihood ratings for each risk

cli_check.what_to_scan: Misuse risk evaluation documentation
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Document at least 5 foreseeable misuse scenarios with analysis and mitigations.
cli_check.warning_message: No misuse risk evaluation found. Art. 9(2)(b) requires evaluating risks from reasonably foreseeable misuse.
negative_signals: No misuse scenarios documented
Only intended use documented without misuse analysis
No mitigations for foreseeable misuse

cli_check.what_to_scan: Test infrastructure for AI system validation before deployment
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Add AI validation tests: bias testing, accuracy benchmarking, robustness checks. Integrate into CI/CD pipeline.
cli_check.warning_message: No AI system testing infrastructure found. Art. 9(7) requires testing high-risk AI systems before market placement with appropriate metrics.
negative_signals: No AI-specific tests in test suite
No CI/CD validation gate for AI model performance
Test directory empty or contains only unit tests without AI validation
positive_signals: Test directory with AI-specific test cases (bias, accuracy, robustness)
CI/CD pipeline includes AI validation step
Test reports with documented metrics and pass/fail thresholds

sdk_implementation.description: Data quality validation middleware for training pipelines
sdk_implementation.middleware_behavior: Pre-training validation: check data completeness, balance, and bias indicators

cli_check.what_to_scan: Training data quality and governance infrastructure
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Add data validation pipeline. Create dataset documentation. Implement bias checking. Use data versioning (DVC or similar).
cli_check.warning_message: No training data governance found. Art. 10 requires providers to use high-quality training data with appropriate governance measures including bias examination.
negative_signals: No data validation in training pipeline
Undocumented training data sources
No bias checking on training data
No data versioning or tracking
positive_signals: Data quality validation scripts in training pipeline

sdk_implementation.description: Bias detection and fairness testing tools analyzing AI outputs across protected characteristics.
sdk_implementation.code_example:
```python
from complior import BiasDetector
detector = BiasDetector(protected_characteristics=['gender','age','ethnicity'])
results = detector.test(predictions=outputs, sensitive_features=demographics)
results.export_report('bias_Q1.pdf')
```
sdk_implementation.middleware_behavior: Batch mode: analyze historical outputs. Live mode: real-time monitoring with threshold alerts.

cli_check.what_to_scan: Model evaluation and data pipeline code
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Add BiasDetector to evaluation pipeline. Test across gender, age, ethnicity.
cli_check.warning_message: WARNING [OBL-004a]: No bias testing detected. Article 10(2)(f) requires bias detection and mitigation.
negative_signals: No bias testing in evaluation
No fairness metrics
Protected characteristics available but untested

cli_check.what_to_scan: Data governance documentation — training data sources and processing
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Run 'ai-comply fix --data-gov' to generate Data Governance template with data provenance fields.

cli_check.what_to_scan: Repository documentation structure for Annex IV compliance
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Create TECHNICAL_DOCUMENTATION.md following Annex IV structure: (1) System description, (2) Development process, (3) Data governance, (4) Risk management, (5) Testing results, (6) Post-market monitoring plan.

README only covers installation, not compliance
No risk management section
No performance metrics documented
positive_signals: Sections covering: system description, development process, data governance, risk management, testing, post-market monitoring

sdk_implementation.description: Middleware intercepts all AI interactions and logs them to compliant storage with integrity protection.
sdk_implementation.middleware_behavior: On every AI API call: capture timestamp, session_id, user_ref (anonymized), input_hash, full output, model_version, confidence, latency, human_oversight_flags.

cli_check.fix_suggestion: Add ComplianceMiddleware.log_interaction() to AI endpoints. Set retention >= 180 days.
cli_check.warning_message: WARNING [OBL-006]: High-risk AI interactions without compliant logging. Article 12 requires automatic event recording.

console.log only
No retention policy
Mutable logs without integrity

sdk_implementation.description: Human-in-the-loop workflow: confidence thresholds, override buttons, review queues, audit trail.
sdk_implementation.middleware_behavior: Check confidence against threshold → route below-threshold decisions to human queue. Log all human decisions.

cli_check.fix_suggestion: Implement an emergency stop endpoint/function that immediately halts AI system processing. Add circuit breaker pattern to AI pipeline.

AI pipeline runs without any override capability
No admin API for system control
AI decisions execute automatically without intervention point

cli_check.what_to_scan: Biometric identification double verification mechanism
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Implement two-step human verification: biometric match must be confirmed by second person before any action is taken.

Direct decision based on biometric match alone
positive_signals: Two-step verification for biometric identification results
Human confirmation required before biometric match action

sdk_implementation.description: Auto-embed machine-readable AI markers into synthetic outputs. Supports C2PA, IPTC, watermark, text provenance.
sdk_implementation.code_example:
```python
from complior import ContentMarker
marker = ContentMarker(regulation='eu-ai-act')
marked_image = marker.mark_image(raw_bytes, model_id='dall-e-3', method=['c2pa','watermark'])
```
sdk_implementation.middleware_behavior: After generation, before delivery: inject C2PA manifest for images, audio watermark for audio/video, provenance metadata for text.

cli_check.what_to_scan: Content generation pipelines
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Add ContentMarker to generation pipeline before delivery.
cli_check.warning_message: WARNING [OBL-016]: AI content generation without machine-readable marking. Article 50(2) requires marking.
negative_signals: AI content generation without marking step
Raw AI output delivered directly

sdk_implementation.description: Embed C2PA/IPTC metadata in AI-generated images
sdk_implementation.code_example:
import { signC2PA } from '@complior/content-marking';
const signedImage = await signC2PA(generatedImage, { model: 'dall-e-3', provider: 'OpenAI' });
sdk_implementation.user_facing_output: AI-generated images contain embedded C2PA metadata indicating artificial origin
sdk_implementation.middleware_behavior: Post-processing: after image generation, inject C2PA manifest with AI provenance data

configuration_options.watermark_visible: boolean, default false (machine-readable only per Art. 50)
configuration_options.provider_attribution: string, provider name in C2PA manifest

cli_check.fix_suggestion: Create COPYRIGHT-POLICY.md documenting your approach to copyright compliance, training data licensing, and rights holder opt-out mechanisms.
cli_check.warning_message: No copyright compliance policy found. Art. 53(1)(d) requires GPAI providers to have a policy to comply with EU copyright law including opt-out mechanisms.
negative_signals: No copyright compliance policy
Training data used without license documentation
No opt-out mechanism for rights holders
positive_signals: Policy describes approach to rights holder opt-out mechanisms

cli_check.what_to_scan: GPAI training data summary publication
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Create TRAINING-DATA-SUMMARY.md describing training data sources, categories, sizes, and processing. Follow the template provided by the AI Office.
cli_check.warning_message: No training data summary found. Art. 53(1)(d) requires GPAI providers to publish a sufficiently detailed summary of training data content.
negative_signals: No training data summary
Training data undisclosed
Data sources described vaguely without actionable information
positive_signals: Summary describes data sources at category level
Publication or public availability of summary

sdk_implementation.description: GPAI systemic risk evaluation and adversarial testing framework

cli_check.what_to_scan: GPAI model evaluation and adversarial testing infrastructure
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Implement: standardized model evaluation benchmarks, red-teaming program, safety testing per OWASP LLM Top 10. Document all results.
cli_check.warning_message: No GPAI model evaluation found. Art. 55(1)(a) requires GPAI providers with systemic risk to perform model evaluations including adversarial testing.
negative_signals: No model evaluation suite
No adversarial or red-team testing
No safety benchmark results
GPAI model deployed without documented evaluation
No systemic risk assessment for high-capability model
positive_signals: Model evaluation suite with standardized benchmarks
Red-teaming or adversarial testing results documented
Safety testing (OWASP LLM Top 10 or equivalent)

cli_check.what_to_scan: GPAI systemic risk cybersecurity measures
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Implement: model access authentication, rate limiting, weight encryption, adversarial testing program, security incident response plan.
cli_check.warning_message: Insufficient cybersecurity for GPAI with systemic risk. Art. 55(1)(d) requires ensuring adequate level of cybersecurity protection.
negative_signals: Model API accessible without authentication
No rate limiting on model inference
Model weights stored unencrypted
No adversarial testing documented
No security incident response plan
positive_signals: Model access protected by authentication and authorization
Incident response plan for model security breaches

sdk_implementation.description: Automated explanation generation for AI decisions using SHAP/LIME/rule extraction.
sdk_implementation.middleware_behavior: On significant decision: generate human-readable explanation with top factors. Include contestation info.

cli_check.what_to_scan: Decision output endpoints
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Add ExplainableDecision wrapper. Include top factors and contestation info.
cli_check.warning_message: WARNING [OBL-024]: Decision endpoint without explanation. Articles 26(11)/86 require explanations for significant AI decisions.
negative_signals: Decision endpoints returning only approve/deny
No explainability library
No appeal mechanism

cli_check.what_to_scan: AI system inventory and version traceability
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Create AI-INVENTORY.md listing all AI systems. Use semantic versioning for models. Maintain CHANGELOG.md for AI changes.
cli_check.warning_message: No AI system inventory or traceability found. Providers must maintain records of AI system versions, modifications, and deployment history.
negative_signals: No AI system inventory documentation
AI models deployed without version tracking
No changelog for AI system modifications
No traceability between model versions and deployments
positive_signals: AI-INVENTORY.md or ai-systems.json listing all AI components
Semantic versioning on AI models and systems
CHANGELOG.md with AI system changes documented
Git tags for AI model releases

cli_check.what_to_scan: Substantial modification indicators in deployer codebase
cli_check.fix_suggestion: Review modifications against Art. 25 criteria. If substantial modification confirmed, assume provider obligations. Document assessment in MODIFICATION-ASSESSMENT.md.
cli_check.warning_message: Potential deployer-becomes-provider situation detected. Art. 25(1)(c): if a deployer substantially modifies a high-risk AI system or changes its intended purpose, they assume provider obligations including conformity assessment.
negative_signals: Custom fine-tuning scripts on provider's base model
Significant post-processing that changes AI system behavior
System used for purpose different from provider's intended purpose
Custom training data applied to modify model behavior
positive_signals: No custom model fine-tuning on top of provider model
Using provider API as-is without significant output modification
Clear documentation that system is used within provider's intended purpose

cli_check.what_to_scan: Open-source GPAI reduced documentation compliance

Missing model card despite GPAI classification
Model exceeds systemic risk threshold but claims open-source exemption
positive_signals: GPAI model card with training methodology description
